description_long: This is a weathered, cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top and a wide, bolted base, typically found in urban environments. The hydrant is predominantly olive green with patches of rust and grime, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. It features two side hose connection outlets, each capped and secured with metal chains, and a central front outlet with a similar cap and chain. The top of the hydrant has a prominent operating nut or cap, used for opening the water valve. The surface is textured with visible bolts and seams, and the metal appears robust and heavy-duty. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A weathered olive green fire hydrant with three capped outlets, metal chains, and a bolted base, showing signs of rust and outdoor wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central outlet with a cap and chain, and the domed top with the operating nut.
description_view_1: Side view displaying both side outlets with caps and chains, and the full circular base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the other side outlet and the central outlet, with visible chains.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the hydrant, with both side outlets and chains visible, but no central outlet.
